cases.title: Predict Binding Sites
cases.description: Given a protein sequence in FASTA format, identify active site residues and binding pocket candidates using sequence-based heuristics and known motif databases.
cases.examplePrompt:
Analyze the following protein sequence and identify potential binding site residues. Highlight any known catalytic motifs and provide the residue positions.

>sp|P04637|P53_HUMAN Cellular tumor antigen p53
MEEPQSDPSVEPPLSQETFSDLWKLLPENNVLSPLPSQAMDDLMLSPDDIEQWFTEDP
cases.exampleResult:
Active Site Residues Identified:
• DNA-binding domain: R175, G245, R248, R249, R273, R282
• Tetramerization domain: residues 325–356

Binding Pocket Candidates:
• Pocket 1 (score 0.89): DNA-binding cleft at residues 176–180, 245–250
• Pocket 2 (score 0.71): Allosteric site near L344

Confidence: High · Based on UniProt P04637 annotations

cases.title: Motif Search
cases.description: Find conserved sequence motifs across a protein family by scanning a list of sequences against known motif databases such as PROSITE or Pfam.
cases.examplePrompt:
Search for conserved motifs in the following kinase domain sequences. Report any PROSITE patterns or Pfam domains found, with positions.

>KinaseA
GXGXXGXXXXK
>KinaseB
GSGSFGTVYK
cases.exampleResult:
Motifs Found:
• PS00107 (Protein kinase ATP-binding): positions 1–11 in KinaseA, 1–10 in KinaseB
• PF00069 (Protein kinase domain): full match in both sequences

Conservation score: 0.94 across 2 sequences
Recommendation: Both sequences belong to the Ser/Thr kinase subfamily.

cases.title: Conservation Analysis
cases.description: Score per-residue conservation from a multiple sequence alignment (MSA). Identifies highly conserved positions likely important for function or structure.
cases.examplePrompt:
Given the following multiple sequence alignment in FASTA format, compute per-residue conservation scores and highlight positions with conservation > 0.8.

>seq1
MKTAYIAKQRQISFVKSHFSRQ
>seq2
MKTAYIAKQRQISFVKSHFSRQ
>seq3
MKTAFIAKQRQISFVKSHFARQ
cases.exampleResult:
Per-residue Conservation Scores (Shannon entropy, normalized):
Pos 1  M: 1.00 ██████████
Pos 2  K: 1.00 ██████████
Pos 3  T: 1.00 ██████████
Pos 5  Y/F: 0.72 ███████
Pos 18 S/A: 0.61 ██████

Highly conserved (>0.8): positions 1–4, 6–17, 19–22
Variable positions: 5, 18

cases.title: Phylogenetic Tree
cases.description: Build an evolutionary tree from a set of aligned protein sequences. Uses neighbor-joining or maximum likelihood methods to infer evolutionary relationships.
cases.examplePrompt:
Construct a phylogenetic tree from the following aligned hemoglobin alpha-chain sequences. Label each branch with bootstrap support values.

>Human
MSLTKTERTIIVSMWAKISTQADTIGTETLERLFLSHPETLEKFDRFKHLKTEAEMK
>Mouse
MSLSADDKTNIKNCWGKIGGHAGEYGAEALERMFLSFPTTKTYFPHFDLSHGSAQVK
>Zebrafish
MSLTAEEKAAVTSLWGKVNVDEVGGEALGRLLVVYPWTQRFFSSFGNLSSATAISGNK
cases.exampleResult:
Phylogenetic Tree (Neighbor-Joining):

┌── Human (bootstrap: 98)
│
├── Mouse (bootstrap: 95)
│
└── Zebrafish (bootstrap: 87)

Tree topology: Human and Mouse form a clade (mammals), Zebrafish is outgroup.
Branch lengths reflect substitutions per site.
Recommendation: Use Maximum Likelihood for more robust support values.

cases.title: Structure Comparison
cases.description: Compute RMSD-based structural alignment between two PDB structures to quantify their 3D similarity and identify structurally equivalent residues.
cases.examplePrompt: Compare the 3D structures of PDB entries 1TUP (p53 DNA-binding domain) and 2OCJ (p53 mutant R175H). Report the RMSD, number of aligned residues, and highlight regions of structural divergence.
cases.exampleResult:
Structural Alignment Results:
• RMSD (all Cα): 0.82 Å over 189 aligned residues
• Sequence identity in aligned region: 99.5%
• Aligned residues: 189 / 194 total

Regions of divergence (RMSD > 2.0 Å):
• Loop L2 (residues 164–194): local RMSD 2.4 Å — mutation R175H causes side-chain repositioning
• Helix H2 (residues 278–289): minor shift of 1.1 Å

Conclusion: Structures are nearly identical globally; local distortion near mutation site.

cases.title: Pocket Detection
cases.description: Identify druggable binding pockets from a PDB structure using geometric cavity detection. Reports pocket volume, depth, and druggability score.
cases.examplePrompt: Analyze the PDB structure 1HSG (HIV-1 protease with bound inhibitor) and identify the top 3 binding pockets. Report their volume, surface area, and druggability score.
cases.exampleResult:
Top Binding Pockets Detected:

1. Active site pocket (druggability: 0.94)
   Volume: 842 Å³ | Surface area: 612 Å²
   Residues: D25, T26, G27, A28, D29, T30 (chain A+B)
   Note: Contains known inhibitor binding site

2. Flap region pocket (druggability: 0.61)
   Volume: 312 Å³ | Surface area: 198 Å²
   Residues: I50, G51, G52 (chain A)

3. Dimer interface (druggability: 0.43)
   Volume: 187 Å³ | Surface area: 143 Å²

cases.title: Contact Map
cases.description: Compute a residue contact map from PDB 3D coordinates. Two residues are in contact if their Cβ atoms are within a specified distance threshold (typically 8 Å).
cases.examplePrompt: Generate a residue contact map for PDB structure 1UBQ (ubiquitin) using an 8 Å Cβ distance threshold. Report the contact density and identify any long-range contacts (|i-j| > 12).
cases.exampleResult:
Contact Map Summary for 1UBQ (76 residues):
• Total contacts: 284 (threshold: 8 Å Cβ-Cβ)
• Contact density: 0.099 contacts/residue²

Long-range contacts (|i-j| > 12): 47 pairs
Notable long-range contacts:
  L8–L50  distance 5.2 Å  (β-strand pairing)
  K11–E34 distance 6.8 Å  (salt bridge)
  V26–I61 distance 7.1 Å  (hydrophobic core)

Sheet topology: β1(2-7)–β2(10-16)–β3(40-45)–β4(48-50)–β5(64-72)

cases.title: Virtual Screening
cases.description: Score a set of small-molecule ligand candidates against a defined protein binding pocket using docking scores and interaction fingerprints.
cases.examplePrompt:
Screen the following 3 SMILES ligands against the HIV-1 protease active site (PDB: 1HSG, pocket 1). Rank by predicted binding affinity and report key interactions.

Ligand A: CC(C)CC1=CC=C(C=C1)C(C)C(=O)O
Ligand B: CN1CCN(CC1)C2=NC3=CC=CC=C3N=C2Cl
Ligand C: OC(=O)C1=CC=CC=C1
cases.exampleResult:
Virtual Screening Results (ranked by docking score):

1. Ligand B  Score: -9.2 kcal/mol
   Key interactions: H-bond with D25 (2.8 Å), hydrophobic with V82, I84
   Predicted Ki: 0.18 μM  ★ Top hit

2. Ligand A  Score: -7.4 kcal/mol
   Key interactions: H-bond with G27 (3.1 Å), van der Waals with L23
   Predicted Ki: 3.2 μM

3. Ligand C  Score: -4.1 kcal/mol
   Key interactions: weak electrostatic with R8
   Predicted Ki: 890 μM  (poor fit)

cases.title: ADMET Prediction
cases.description: Predict absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, and toxicity (ADMET) properties for a small molecule using its SMILES string.
cases.examplePrompt:
Predict the ADMET properties for the following drug candidate:

SMILES: CN1CCN(CC1)C2=NC3=CC=CC=C3N=C2Cl
Compound name: Clozapine

Report Lipinski's Rule of Five compliance, predicted solubility, blood-brain barrier penetration, CYP450 inhibition, and hERG toxicity risk.
cases.exampleResult:
ADMET Prediction for Clozapine (CN1CCN(CC1)C2=NC3=CC=CC=C3N=C2Cl):

Lipinski RO5: ✓ PASS
  MW: 326.8 Da | logP: 3.2 | HBD: 1 | HBA: 4 | RotBonds: 2

Absorption:
  Caco-2 permeability: 18.4 nm/s (high)
  Oral bioavailability: ~60% (predicted)

Distribution:
  BBB penetration: HIGH (logBB = 0.31) — consistent with CNS activity
  Plasma protein binding: 97%

Metabolism:
  CYP1A2 substrate: YES (major)
  CYP3A4 inhibitor: moderate risk

Toxicity:
  hERG inhibition: MODERATE risk (IC50 ~1.2 μM)
  Ames mutagenicity: NEGATIVE
  Hepatotoxicity: LOW risk

cases.title: Protein Lookup
cases.description: Look up a protein by UniProt accession to retrieve its name, function, organism, sequence length, and associated gene names.
cases.examplePrompt: What is the function of P38398?
cases.exampleResult:
UniProt Entry: P38398 — BRCA1_HUMAN

Protein name: Breast cancer type 1 susceptibility protein
Gene: BRCA1 (also known as FANCS, PPP1R53, RNF53)
Organism: Homo sapiens (Human)
Sequence length: 1863 amino acids
Chromosome: 17q21.31

Function:
Involved in DNA repair in response to double-strand breaks. Acts as a tumor suppressor. Has E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase activity. Required for maintenance of genomic stability. Participates in the BRCA1-PALB2-BRCA2 triple-BRCT domain complex.

Status: Reviewed (Swiss-Prot)

cases.title: Domain Architecture
cases.description: Retrieve domain and family annotations from InterPro for a protein, including Pfam, PROSITE, and CDD matches with residue coordinates.
cases.examplePrompt: What domains and families does EGFR (P00533) contain? Show domain coordinates.
cases.exampleResult:
InterPro Domains for P00533 (EGFR_HUMAN):

Domains found: 6

1. IPR009030 — Furin-like cysteine rich domain
   Pfam: PF00757; positions 180–309
   Source: Pfam

2. IPR009030 — Furin-like cysteine rich domain (2nd)
   Pfam: PF00757; positions 348–481
   Source: Pfam

3. IPR013098 — EGF receptor, L domain
   Pfam: PF07714; positions 57–167
   Source: Pfam

4. IPR000719 — Protein kinase domain
   Pfam: PF00069; positions 712–979
   PROSITE: PS50011
   Source: Pfam, PROSITE

5. IPR001245 — Serine-threonine/tyrosine-protein kinase
   CDD: cd05052; positions 719–972
   Source: CDD

6. IPR017441 — Protein kinase, ATP binding site
   PROSITE: PS00107; positions 730–752
   Source: PROSITE

cases.title: Protein Family Classification
cases.description: Classify a protein into PANTHER families and subfamilies to understand its evolutionary relationships and protein class.
cases.examplePrompt: Which PANTHER family and subfamily does BRCA1 (P38398) belong to? What is its protein class?
cases.exampleResult:
PANTHER Classification for P38398 (BRCA1_HUMAN):

Family: PTHR11289
Family name: BREAST CANCER TYPE 1 SUSCEPTIBILITY PROTEIN

Subfamily: PTHR11289:SF62
Subfamily name: Breast cancer type 1 susceptibility protein

Protein class:
• PC00064 — DNA binding protein
• PC00260 — Tumor suppressor

GO Slim annotations:
• Biological process: DNA repair, response to DNA damage
• Molecular function: ubiquitin-protein transferase activity
• Cellular component: nucleus

Evolutionary notes:
Member of the BRCA1 C-terminus (BRCT) superfamily. Conserved from vertebrates to invertebrates. Human protein is 76% identical to mouse ortholog.

cases.title: GO Term Annotations
cases.description: Retrieve Gene Ontology annotations for a protein from QuickGO, organized by molecular function, biological process, and cellular component.
cases.examplePrompt: What are the Gene Ontology annotations for TP53 (P04637)? Show all three GO aspects.
cases.exampleResult:
GO Annotations for P04637 (P53_HUMAN) via QuickGO:

Molecular Function (MF):
• GO:0003700 — DNA-binding transcription factor activity (IDA)
• GO:0004871 — signal transducer activity (IDA)
• GO:0008134 — transcription factor binding (IPI)
• GO:0046872 — metal ion binding (IEA)
• GO:0042802 — identical protein binding (IPI)

Biological Process (BP):
• GO:0006351 — transcription by RNA polymerase II (IDA)
• GO:0006978 — DNA damage response, signal transduction (IDA)
• GO:0006915 — apoptotic process (IDA)
• GO:0016045 — detection of bacterium (IDA)
• GO:0045893 — positive regulation of transcription (TAS)

Cellular Component (CC):
• GO:0005654 — nucleoplasm (IDA)
• GO:0005813 — centrosome (IDA)
• GO:0005829 — cytosol (IDA)
• GO:0016604 — nuclear body (IDA)

Total: 47 annotations (18 manually reviewed)

cases.title: PTM Sites
cases.description: Retrieve known post-translational modification sites for a protein from UniProt PTM annotations, including phosphorylation, ubiquitination, and acetylation.
cases.examplePrompt: What phosphorylation and ubiquitination sites are known for EGFR (P00533)?
cases.exampleResult:
PTM Sites for P00533 (EGFR_HUMAN) from UniProt:

Phosphorylation sites (42 total):
• Y1068 — autophosphorylation (major site); recruits GRB2 and SHC1
• Y1086 — autophosphorylation; recruits SH2 domain proteins
• Y1148 — autophosphorylation; SHC recruitment
• Y1173 — autophosphorylation; SHC/PLCG1 recruitment
• T669  — phosphorylated by ERK1/2 (negative feedback)
• S695  — phosphorylated by PKC
• T693  — phosphorylated by CK2

Ubiquitination sites (8 total):
• K692  — ubiquitinated by CBL E3 ligase (degradation signal)
• K708  — monoubiquitination by CBL
• K730  — polyubiquitination (K48-linked, proteasomal degradation)

Acetylation sites (3 total):
• K689  — acetyltransferase p300/CBP

Note: PTM data sourced from UniProt experimental annotations (EXP) and similarity-based inference (ISS).

cases.title: Sequence Properties
cases.description: Compute physical and chemical properties of a protein sequence locally: molecular weight, isoelectric point, GRAVY score, and amino acid composition.
cases.examplePrompt:
Analyze this protein sequence and report its molecular weight, isoelectric point, GRAVY score, and amino acid composition:

>test_protein
MKTAYIAKQRQISFVKSHFSRQLESSPGNFQTPYGIDRNSTREACLNLLSVAADSQEWEQVEGSFGELLNALG
cases.exampleResult:
Sequence Analysis Results:

Length: 74 amino acids

Molecular weight: 8,214.4 Da
Isoelectric point (pI): 5.82
GRAVY score: -0.431 (hydrophilic)

Amino acid composition (top 5):
  L (Leucine):    9 residues (12.2%)
  A (Alanine):    7 residues (9.5%)
  G (Glycine):    5 residues (6.8%)
  S (Serine):     5 residues (6.8%)
  E (Glutamate):  5 residues (6.8%)

Extinction coefficients:
  At 280 nm (oxidized cysteines):  2,980 M⁻¹cm⁻¹
  At 280 nm (reduced cysteines):   2,980 M⁻¹cm⁻¹

No cysteines detected — no disulfide bonds expected.

cases.title: ProtParam Analysis
cases.description: Run a full ExPASy ProtParam-style analysis including instability index, signal peptide prediction, and transmembrane helix detection.
cases.examplePrompt:
Run a full ProtParam analysis on the TP53 N-terminal transactivation domain sequence and report all physicochemical properties including stability.

>sp|P04637|P53_HUMAN transactivation domain
MEEPQSDPSVEPPLSQETFSDLWKLLPENNVLSPLPSQAMDDLMLSPDDIEQWFTEDP
cases.exampleResult:
ExPASy ProtParam Analysis:

Sequence length: 61 amino acids
Molecular weight: 7,024.0 Da
Isoelectric point (pI): 3.93 (acidic)

Amino acid composition:
  D (Asp): 7 (11.5%)  E (Glu): 5 (8.2%)
  P (Pro): 7 (11.5%)  L (Leu): 6 (9.8%)
  S (Ser): 5 (8.2%)   W (Trp): 1 (1.6%)

Instability index: 62.3 → UNSTABLE
(Proteins with index > 40 are predicted unstable in vitro)

Aliphatic index: 59.5 (relatively low — consistent with intrinsically disordered region)

GRAVY: -1.066 (strongly hydrophilic — consistent with disordered transactivation domain)

Extinction coefficient at 280 nm: 5,500 M⁻¹cm⁻¹ (1 Trp, 0 Tyr, 0 Cys)

Signal peptide: NOT detected
Transmembrane helices: 0 predicted

Note: This region is a well-characterized intrinsically disordered domain.

cases.title: CATH Domain Classification
cases.description: Query the CATH database to get structural domain classification for a protein, including the four-level CATH hierarchy: Class, Architecture, Topology, and Homology.
cases.examplePrompt: Classify the structural domains of the EGFR kinase domain. Use UniProt accession P00533.
cases.exampleResult:
CATH Classification for P00533 (EGFR_HUMAN):

Domain 1 — Kinase domain (residues 712–979)
CATH code: 3.30.200.20
  Class:        3 — Mixed alpha/beta
  Architecture: 3.30 — Two-layer sandwich
  Topology:     3.30.200 — Adenylate Kinase; chain A
  Homology:     3.30.200.20 — Phosphorylase Kinase; domain 1

Related PDB entries in same H-group:
  1IVO, 3NJP, 7JXR (all EGFR kinase structures)
  Number of structures in homology group: 312

Domain 2 — Receptor L domain (residues 57–167)
CATH code: 2.60.40.10
  Class:        2 — Mostly beta
  Architecture: 2.60 — Beta sandwich
  Topology:     2.60.40 — Immunoglobulin-like fold
  Homology:     2.60.40.10 — Immunoglobulins

Functional site: The ATP-binding cleft lies between the N-lobe (β-strands) and C-lobe (α-helices) of domain 1.

cases.title: Clinical Variant Search
cases.description: Search ClinVar for clinically significant variants associated with a gene, including pathogenic calls and associated disease conditions.
cases.examplePrompt: What pathogenic and likely pathogenic variants are reported in ClinVar for BRCA2?
cases.exampleResult:
ClinVar Results for BRCA2:

Total variants: 14,892 submissions
Pathogenic: 1,204
Likely pathogenic: 687
VUS (uncertain significance): 8,341
Likely benign: 2,103
Benign: 2,557

Top pathogenic variants:
• c.5946delT (p.Ser1982Argfs) — Hereditary breast/ovarian cancer
  ClinVar ID: 52555 | Review status: ★★★★ (practice guideline)
• c.6275_6276delTT — Fanconi anemia D1 / Breast-ovarian cancer
  ClinVar ID: 38071 | Review status: ★★★ (expert panel)
• c.1813dup (p.Thr605Asnfs) — Hereditary breast/ovarian cancer
  ClinVar ID: 52558

Associated conditions: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome, Fanconi anemia complementation group D1, Familial cancer of breast

cases.title: SNP Lookup by rsID
cases.description: Look up a specific SNP by its rsID to retrieve genomic position, alleles, population frequencies, and clinical significance.
cases.examplePrompt: Look up rs121434568. What are its alleles, allele frequency, and clinical significance?
cases.exampleResult:
dbSNP Entry: rs121434568

Variant type: SNV (single nucleotide variant)
Gene: EGFR
HGVS (coding): NM_005228.5:c.2573T>G
HGVS (protein): NP_005219.2:p.Leu858Arg (L858R)

Genomic location: chr7:55,191,822 (GRCh38)
Ref allele: T
Alt allele: G

Allele frequency:
  gnomAD overall: 0.000004 (4 in 1,000,000)
  gnomAD East Asian: 0.000012
  Note: primarily observed as somatic mutation in lung cancer

Clinical significance: PATHOGENIC
  Condition: Non-small cell lung carcinoma (somatic driver)
  Review status: ★★★ (criteria provided, multiple submitters)

Somatic context: Present in ~38% of EGFR-mutant NSCLCs; sensitizing mutation for erlotinib/gefitinib

cases.title: Gene Constraint Metrics
cases.description: Check gnomAD constraint metrics for a gene to understand its tolerance to loss-of-function and missense variants in the general population.
cases.examplePrompt: How constrained is TP53 against loss-of-function variants? Show pLI, LOEUF, and missense constraint.
cases.exampleResult:
gnomAD Constraint Metrics for TP53:

Loss-of-function constraint:
  pLI score: 1.00 (probability of being LoF intolerant)
  LOEUF: 0.27 (observed/expected upper bound fraction)
  → Highly constrained — TP53 is in the top 10% most LoF-intolerant genes
  Expected LoF variants: 12.4 | Observed: 3 | O/E ratio: 0.24

Missense constraint:
  z-score: 3.09 (highly constrained for missense)
  Expected missense: 187.3 | Observed: 90 | O/E: 0.48

Copy number variation:
  pHaplo: 0.97 (haploinsufficient)
  pTriplo: 0.41 (moderate triplosensitivity)

Interpretation: TP53 is one of the most constrained genes in the human genome. Nearly all observed LoF variants are de novo or heterozygous cancer somatic mutations — homozygous germline LoF is lethal.

cases.title: GWAS Associations
cases.description: Search the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog for genome-wide association study hits linked to a gene, including traits, p-values, and odds ratios.
cases.examplePrompt: What GWAS associations have been reported for the APOE gene?
cases.exampleResult:
GWAS Catalog Associations for APOE:

Total GWAS hits: 156

Top associations by significance:
1. Alzheimer's disease (late-onset)
   Lead SNP: rs429358 (APOE ε4 allele, C allele)
   p-value: 1×10⁻³⁰⁰ | OR: 3.7 | Sample: 1,126,563 individuals
   PMID: 35379992 (2022)

2. LDL cholesterol levels
   Lead SNP: rs7412 (APOE ε2 allele, T allele)
   p-value: 3×10⁻⁴²⁰ | Beta: -15.3 mg/dL | Sample: 1,320,016

3. Coronary artery disease
   Lead SNP: rs445925
   p-value: 4×10⁻¹⁸ | OR: 1.18 | Sample: 547,261

4. Total cholesterol
   Lead SNP: rs429358 | p-value: 2×10⁻³⁸⁰

5. Longevity / lifespan
   Lead SNP: rs4420638 | p-value: 8×10⁻¹² | Sample: 390,000

Note: APOE ε4 (rs429358-C + rs7412-C haplotype) is the strongest known genetic risk factor for sporadic Alzheimer's disease.

cases.title: Target-Disease Associations
cases.description: Query Open Targets Platform for disease associations and drug tractability for a target gene.
cases.examplePrompt: What are the top disease associations for BRAF on Open Targets? Include tractability and known drug count.
cases.exampleResult:
Open Targets: BRAF (ENSG00000157764)

Top disease associations (by overall score):
1. Melanoma                    score: 0.97  (genetic + somatic + drug evidence)
2. Colorectal carcinoma        score: 0.91
3. Non-small cell lung cancer  score: 0.88
4. Thyroid gland carcinoma     score: 0.85
5. Hairy cell leukemia         score: 0.79

Drug tractability:
  Small molecule: HIGH (clinical precedence — vemurafenib, dabrafenib approved)
  Antibody: LOW
  Other modalities: PREDICTED tractable

Approved drugs: 6 (vemurafenib, dabrafenib, encorafenib + MEK inhibitor combinations)
Clinical candidates: 23 additional compounds in trials

Genetic evidence: BRAF V600E somatic mutation — present in 50% of melanomas, 10% of colorectal cancers, 60% of papillary thyroid cancers

cases.title: Disease-Gene Associations
cases.description: Search DisGeNET for diseases associated with a gene, with evidence scores from curated and text-mined sources.

cases.exampleResult:

cases.title: OMIM Disease Lookup
cases.description: Look up OMIM entries for Mendelian diseases associated with a gene via NCBI Gene linkage.
cases.examplePrompt: What Mendelian diseases does OMIM associate with FGFR3?
cases.exampleResult:
OMIM Entries for FGFR3 (Gene ID: 2261):

OMIM Gene entry: *134934 (FGFR3)

Associated phenotypes:
1. Achondroplasia (#100800)
   Inheritance: Autosomal dominant
   Mutation: G380R (gain-of-function, constitutive activation)
   Frequency: 1/25,000 live births — most common form of dwarfism

2. Thanatophoric dysplasia, type I (#187600)
   Inheritance: Autosomal dominant (de novo)
   Mutation: R248C, Y373C (severe gain-of-function)
   Lethal perinatally

3. Thanatophoric dysplasia, type II (#187601)
   Mutation: K650E (kinase domain)

4. Hypochondroplasia (#146000)
   Mutation: N540K (milder than achondroplasia)

5. Bladder cancer, somatic (#109800)
   Somatic mutations in ~70% of superficial bladder tumors (S249C most common)

6. LADD syndrome (#149730)
   Inheritance: Autosomal dominant; features: lacrimal gland aplasia

All pathogenic FGFR3 mutations are gain-of-function; loss-of-function is embryonic lethal.

cases.title: Cancer Mutation Landscape
cases.description: Query cBioPortal for the cancer mutation landscape of a gene across studies, including mutation types and alteration frequency.
cases.examplePrompt: What is the mutation frequency and top mutation types for KRAS across cancer types in cBioPortal?
cases.exampleResult:
cBioPortal: KRAS (Entrez ID: 3845)

Gene overview:
Cytoband: 12p12.1 | Type: protein-coding
Alteration frequency: 20% across all cancer studies (535+ studies)

Top cancer types by alteration frequency:
1. Pancreatic adenocarcinoma:    92% (G12D 36%, G12V 34%, G12R 16%)
2. Colorectal adenocarcinoma:    42% (G12D 37%, G12V 26%, G13D 19%)
3. Non-small cell lung cancer:   31% (G12C 40%, G12V 21%, G12D 17%)
4. Endometrial carcinoma:        22% (G12D most common)
5. Ovarian serous adenocarcinoma: 12%

Mutation types (all cancer):
  Missense: 95.4%
  Truncating: 2.1%
  Amplification: 1.8%
  Other: 0.7%

Top hotspot residues: G12 (78% of mutations), G13 (14%), Q61 (5%)

Note: KRAS G12C is the target of sotorasib (AMG 510) — first approved KRAS inhibitor.

cases.title: Drug-Target Interactions
cases.description: Search ChEMBL for approved drugs and clinical candidates targeting a specific protein, including mechanism of action and IC50 values.
cases.examplePrompt: What approved drugs and clinical candidates target VEGFR2 (KDR/FLK-1)?
cases.exampleResult:
ChEMBL Drug-Target Results for VEGFR2 (KDR, CHEMBL279):

Approved drugs (Phase 4): 8
1. Sunitinib (CHEMBL535)   — multi-kinase inhibitor; IC50 9 nM (KDR)
   Approved: renal cell carcinoma, GIST; oral
2. Sorafenib (CHEMBL1336)  — RAF/VEGFR/PDGFR inhibitor; IC50 90 nM
   Approved: HCC, RCC, thyroid cancer
3. Axitinib (CHEMBL1289926)— selective VEGFR1/2/3 inhibitor; IC50 0.2 nM
   Approved: renal cell carcinoma
4. Pazopanib (CHEMBL477772)— VEGFR/PDGFR/FGFR; IC50 10 nM
   Approved: RCC, soft tissue sarcoma
5. Cabozantinib (CHEMBL2105717) — VEGFR2/MET/RET; IC50 0.035 nM
   Approved: RCC, HCC, thyroid

Clinical candidates (Phase 1–3): 47 additional compounds
Top Phase 3: tivozanib (IC50 0.16 nM, approved in EU)

Mechanism: All are ATP-competitive kinase inhibitors binding the VEGFR2 activation loop.

cases.title: Reactome Pathway Search
cases.description: Query Reactome for biological pathways associated with a protein, including sub-pathway hierarchy and diagram availability.
cases.examplePrompt: What are the top biological pathways that MTOR participates in according to Reactome?
cases.exampleResult:
Reactome Pathways for MTOR (P42345):

Top-level pathways: 12
Total pathway entries (incl. sub-pathways): 89

Top pathways:
1. R-HSA-165159 — mTOR signalling
   Species: Homo sapiens | Diagram: available
   Sub-pathways: mTORC1 activation (R-HSA-166208), mTORC2 activation (R-HSA-5663202)

2. R-HSA-2219528 — PI3K/AKT Signaling in Cancer
   Top-level: Signal Transduction | Diagram: available

3. R-HSA-5633007 — Regulation of TP53 Degradation
   mTOR activates MDM2 via S6K1 → TP53 ubiquitination

4. R-HSA-9612973 — Autophagy
   mTORC1 phosphorylates ULK1 to suppress autophagy initiation
   Sub-pathway: Mitophagy (R-HSA-9612973)

5. R-HSA-1257604 — PIP3 activates AKT signaling
   Upstream of mTOR; AKT → TSC1/2 → Rheb → mTORC1

Note: mTOR acts as a central sensor of nutrient availability, energy status, and growth factor signals.

cases.title: WikiPathways Search
cases.description: Search WikiPathways for biological pathways involving a specific gene, with pathway IDs and species information.
cases.examplePrompt: Which WikiPathways involve INSR (the insulin receptor gene)? List pathways with IDs.
cases.exampleResult:
WikiPathways Results for INSR:

Total pathways found: 24

Top pathways (Homo sapiens):
1. WP481 — Insulin Signaling

2. WP4723 — SARS-CoV-2 and COVID-19 Pathway
   INSR involved in metabolic dysregulation in COVID-19

3. WP23 — B cell receptor signaling pathway

4. WP4396 — Nonalcoholic fatty liver disease

6. WP4963 — COVID-19 adverse outcome pathway
   Metabolic complications

Other species: Mus musculus (8 pathways), Rattus norvegicus (5 pathways), Danio rerio (3)

cases.title: Protein Interaction Partners
cases.description: Query STRING for top protein-protein interaction partners with combined confidence scores.
cases.examplePrompt: Who are the top 8 interaction partners of TP53 in STRING?
cases.exampleResult:
STRING PPI Partners for TP53 (P04637, taxon: 9606):

Top partners (by combined interaction score):

1. MDM2    combined: 0.999  (textmining: 0.99, experiments: 0.99, co-expression: 0.78)
   MDM2 is the primary negative regulator of TP53 — E3 ubiquitin ligase targeting p53 for degradation

2. MDM4    combined: 0.996  (textmining: 0.98, experiments: 0.98)
   MDMX/MDM4 — p53 inhibitor, forms heterodimer with MDM2

3. ATM     combined: 0.993  (experiments: 0.98, textmining: 0.97)
   ATM phosphorylates TP53 at S15 in response to double-strand breaks

4. CDKN2A  combined: 0.987  (textmining: 0.96, co-expression: 0.88)
   ARF (p14ARF) sequesters MDM2 → stabilizes p53

5. CHEK2   combined: 0.981  (experiments: 0.97)
   CHK2 phosphorylates TP53 at S20

6. EP300   combined: 0.977  (experiments: 0.95, textmining: 0.94)
   p300 acetyltransferase activates TP53 transcriptional activity

7. TP63    combined: 0.972  (co-expression: 0.91, textmining: 0.88)
   p63 — p53 family member, competitive binding

8. BRCA1   combined: 0.968  (experiments: 0.94)
   Co-activates TP53 transcription; DNA damage response

cases.title: Curated Molecular Interactions
cases.description: Retrieve curated binary protein interactions from IntAct (EBI), including detection methods and MI scores.
cases.examplePrompt: What curated interactions are known for MDM2 in IntAct? Show top partners and experimental methods.
cases.exampleResult:
IntAct Interactions for MDM2 (Q00987):

Total curated interactions: 1,247
Unique interactors: 189

Top interaction partners:
1. TP53 (P04637)   — 312 records
   Methods: anti-bait co-IP (124), two-hybrid (87), fluorescence microscopy (41)
   MI score: 0.87 (high confidence)
   Key interface: MDM2 N-terminal domain (aa 25–109) binds p53 transactivation domain

2. MDM4 (O15151)   — 94 records
   Methods: co-IP (61), GST pull-down (21), FRET (12)
   MDM2-MDM4 RING domain heterodimerization → enhanced p53 ubiquitination

3. USP7 (Q93009)   — 67 records
   MDM2 deubiquitinase; USP7 removes K48-linked ubiquitin from MDM2 and p53

4. DAXX (Q9UER7)   — 34 records
   DAXX/HAUSP complex; regulates MDM2 stability

5. CDKN2A (P42771) — 28 records (p14ARF)
   ARF binds MDM2 RING domain → nucleolar sequestration → p53 stabilization

Detection method breakdown:
  Co-immunoprecipitation: 487 records (39%)
  Two-hybrid: 312 records (25%)
  Pull-down: 198 records (16%)
  Other: 250 records (20%)

cases.title: Tissue Expression (GTEx)
cases.description: Query the GTEx Portal for tissue-specific gene expression levels, showing median TPM values across human tissues.
cases.examplePrompt: In which tissues is ACE2 most highly expressed according to GTEx? Show the top 10 tissues.
cases.exampleResult:
GTEx Expression for ACE2:

Top 10 tissues by median TPM:
1. Small intestine (terminal ileum):  105.3 TPM
2. Testis:                             91.6 TPM
3. Kidney cortex:                      48.2 TPM
4. Thyroid:                            30.9 TPM
5. Heart — left ventricle:             22.1 TPM
6. Heart — atrial appendage:           19.8 TPM
7. Adipose — visceral (omentum):       18.4 TPM
8. Colon — transverse:                 14.2 TPM
9. Liver:                              12.7 TPM
10. Breast — mammary tissue:           11.3 TPM

Low expression tissues:
  Lung: 2.1 TPM (unexpectedly low for SARS-CoV-2 entry receptor)
  Brain regions: < 1.0 TPM
  Blood: 0.3 TPM

Note: GTEx v8 data, 17,382 samples across 54 tissues from 948 donors.
Despite low lung TPM, single-cell studies show ACE2 is highly expressed in type II pneumocytes.

cases.title: Protein Atlas Expression
cases.description: Retrieve tissue expression, IHC staining results, and subcellular localization from the Human Protein Atlas.
cases.examplePrompt: Where is CDH1 (E-cadherin) expressed according to the Human Protein Atlas? Include subcellular localization and cancer expression.
cases.exampleResult:
Human Protein Atlas: CDH1 (P12830, E-Cadherin)

RNA expression (HPA RNA consensus dataset):
  Highest: small intestine, liver, kidney, thyroid
  Tissue specificity: Enhanced in epithelial tissues

IHC protein expression (antibody HPA001765):
  Strong staining: liver hepatocytes, kidney tubules, small intestine enterocytes
  Moderate staining: pancreas, breast, skin
  Negative: brain, blood cells, skeletal muscle

Subcellular localization:
  Primary: Cell junctions (adherens junctions) — ★★★
  Secondary: Plasma membrane — ★★★
  Note: Forms Ca²⁺-dependent homophilic trans-dimers at E-cadherin-based adherens junctions

Cancer atlas:
  Normal-to-cancer expression change:
  • Colorectal cancer: LOW/absent in 62% of cases (EMT marker — loss = poor prognosis)
  • Gastric cancer: LOW in 45% (CDH1 germline mutations → hereditary diffuse gastric cancer)
  • Breast cancer: absent in triple-negative subtypes
  • Lung adenocarcinoma: generally retained

cases.title: NCBI Gene Search
cases.description: Search NCBI Gene (Entrez) for gene information including official symbol, aliases, chromosome location, and function summary.
cases.examplePrompt: Search NCBI Gene for PTEN. Report its gene ID, chromosome location, aliases, and official summary.
cases.exampleResult:
NCBI Gene: PTEN

Gene ID: 5728
Official symbol: PTEN
Official full name: phosphatase and tensin homolog
Also known as: BZS, DEC, GLM2, MHAM, MMAC1, PTEN1, TEP1, 10q23del

Organism: Homo sapiens
Chromosome: 10 | Location: 10q23.31
Coordinates (GRCh38): chr10:87,863,113–87,971,930
Orientation: minus strand

Gene type: protein-coding
MIM: 601728

Summary:
This gene encodes a phosphatidylinositol-3,4,5-trisphosphate 3-phosphatase and dual-specificity protein phosphatase. The protein, a tumor suppressor, antagonizes the PI3K-AKT-PKB signaling pathway by dephosphorylating PIP3 to PIP2, which inhibits cell growth and migration. PTEN is the second most commonly mutated gene in human cancer after TP53. Germline mutations cause PTEN hamartoma tumor syndrome (Cowden disease).

Expression: ubiquitous (brain, prostate, breast most relevant)
Interactome partners: AKT1, PIK3CA, TP53, MDM2, MAGI2

cases.title: Multi-Source Literature Search

cases.exampleResult:

  ✓ Semantic Scholar — 567 results

After DOI deduplication: 498 unique records
